title: × Elyhordeum piperi
taxonomy: × Elyhordeum piperi is a hybrid of Elymus and Hordeum. It is classified as a species within the genus Elyhordeum, which is part of the family Poaceae. The classification of × Elyhordeum piperi is not well-documented, but it is believed to be a rare or unusual hybrid. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification of this plant. The classification is likely to be subject to change as more information becomes available.
uses: × Elyhordeum piperi, also known as Piper's spelt or piper's wheat, is not a well-documented plant. However, the genus Elymus and the genus Hordeum, both of which are related to × Elyhordeum piperi, have been used in traditional medicine. The Elymus genus is known for its use in treating respiratory issues and as a diuretic. Hordeum, or barley, has been used to treat various conditions including digestive issues and skin problems. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses of × Elyhordeum piperi specifically.
